title: The Simon & Garfunkel Story
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $69 
VIP 1&2: The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience  $172-$182 
The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Includes: 

One Ticket with in the first 10 rows
A private 20-minute acoustic performance with the lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story for VIP attendees only, featuring exclusive Simon & Garfunkel songs not performed in the main show. “Stripped back, with one guitar and two voices… singing in harmony.”
A Meet & Greet and photo opportunity with the two lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story.
A limited edition, collectible VIP laminate.

VIP UPGRADE: The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Upgrade $113  
The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Upgrade* Includes: 

A private 20-minute acoustic performance with the lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story for VIP attendees only, featuring exclusive Simon & Garfunkel songs not performed in the main show. “Stripped back, with one guitar and two voices… singing in harmony.”
A Meet & Greet and photo opportunity with the two lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story.
A limited edition, collectible VIP laminate.

*Upgrade packages DO NOT INCLUDE TICKETS. A ticket is required to enter the venue. 
Now seen in 50 countries worldwide, The Simon and Garfunkel Story is a critically acclaimed concert style theatre show about two young boys from Queens, New York who went on to become the world’s most successful music duo of all time.  Using state of the art video projection, incredible lighting, and a full live band, The Simon & Garfunkel Story is a moving and powerful concert featuring all the hits such as “Mrs. Robinson,” “Bridge Over Troubled Water,” “Homeward Bound,” “Scarborough Fair,” “The Boxer,” “The Sound Of Silence,” and many more. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Fact vs Fiction: The Reality of a Missing Persons Investigation
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Community, Presentation, Adults, Seniors

We've all read it in novels and watched it on TV: a person vanishes and a lone detective solves the case in 48 hours. But what actually happens when a Pennsylvanian goes missing? Join us for an evening with State Trooper James Grothey and officers from the Manheim Township Police Department as they pull back the curtain on the real-world investigative process.From the critical first hours and to the role of digital forensics and community alerts, learn how law enforcement navigates the complexities of search and recovery. Discover the truth behind "The First 48," the legal hurdles of the job, and how real life is much more intricate than fiction. Registration is encouraged

In this facilitated session (particularly valuable to those with executive function challenges), participants ages 18-24 can bring homework, job applications, life admin tasks, or creative projects and work quietly alongside others during guided focus periods. location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:

title: The Temptations & The Four Tops
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $79 $89 $99 $109
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, August 13 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, August 15 at 10:00 a.m. 
Often referred to as American Music Royalty, The Temptations are world-renowned superstars of entertainment, revered for their phenomenal catalog of music and prolific career.  Thanks to their fine-tuned choreography, and even finer harmonies, The Temptations became one of the most successful acts to record for Motown Records.  During the group’s six-decade career, these Grammy winners have charted numerous #1 hits including “My Girl,” “Ain’t Too Proud to Beg,” “Get Ready,” “Just My Imagination,” and “Papa Was a Rollin’ Stone,” among many others. 
The Four Tops teamed up in high school and spent over four decades without a single personnel change to become one of Motown’s most consistent hit makers.  “Baby I Need Your Loving,” “I Can’t Help Myself (Sugar Pie, Honey Bunch),” “Reach Out (I’ll Be There),” “Standing in the Shadows of Love,” and “Ain’t No Woman (Like the One I’ve Got)” are just some of their classic hits. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Annual Garage Sale Day 1
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Searching for a massive, budget-friendly yard sale in or near Lancaster? Look no further!
Join us at Church of the Apostles UCC for our annual garage sale held on the Friday and Saturday following Easter. Be prepared to shop early, as there’s always a long line before doors open on Friday! 
This years sale is set for April 10th from 8 AM – 2 PM & April 11th from 8 AM – 1 PM in and around Fellowship Hall. Our address is 1850 Marietta Avenue, Lancaster PA. Be sure to follow signs for Fellowship Hall! 
All are welcome to attend this fun-filled annual event. We have many low-priced items for you to browse through including clothing, collectables, home furnishings, jewelry, sports items, tools, toys and so much more! Food options include hotdogs, sodas and baked goods on Friday and Saturday! Ample parking, including accessible parking, is available on both days. 
If you are interested in donating items, you can do so the week of the sale, April 6th, 7th, & 8th, from 9 AM – 9 PM, or April 9th, from 9 AM – 12 PM, by the Fellowship Hall doors. For additional information, please feel free to contact us at office@apostlesucc.org or by calling (717) 392-5718.

title: Together, They Made the Modern American Presidency
location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:
Presentation, Teens, Adults, Seniors

Adult program, older teens are welcome. Please register for each session.Dr. Robert Frick, local historian, author, and retired Lampeter Strasburg School District Superintendent, has developed a number of lecture series to inform and entertain you with objective, non-partisan content.  This series focuses on the modern American Presidency.The framers of the U.S. Constitution were wary of the Executive Branch, the most likely source of tyranny in the government. Having escaped the authority of a king, they wanted no more of it or an executive who would dominate the new government; thus, the careful wording of Article II. As leaders, Presidents need to communicate with the public, build a team, be assertive, create overarching goals, process information, and manage their emotions. These qualities come in varying degrees and sundry types with each new President. Historians believe it takes 20 years to properly evaluate a presidency, therefore this series culminates with the 43rd president.February 27 –  FDR, TrumanMarch 6 – Eisenhower, KennedyMarch 13 –  Johnson, NixonMarch 20 –  Ford, CarterMarch 27 – Reagan, GHW BushApril 3 - Library closed April 10 – Clinton, GW Bush

Join us on Friday, April 10th as we create, test, and evaluate our very own Wind Cars. Why Video Games and Esports?
Esports is one of the fastest-growing career fields in the world, offering opportunities not only as players but also in areas like computer programming, digital art, broadcasting, marketing, and game development. Colleges and universities, including local institutions, now offer esports teams, scholarships, and academic pathways that turn a passion for gaming into meaningful careers.

location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $64 $74 $84 
VIP: Alton Brown VIP Experience: $200 
Includes: 

One ticket for admission
A special VIP experience one hour before show time.
It will include a brief Q&A session in the Theatre.
A signed copy of Alton’s book.

*There will be no individual photos or autographs, however, photos will be allowed during the experience.  Video is strictly prohibited. 
*If you purchase a VIP Experience, AMT will notify you by email prior to the event to give you additional instructions. 
Although he’s keeping the details under wraps, Brown has said that with An Evening of Alton Brown he’s “stirring up some twists on his greatest hits,” and “spicing things up with new demonstrations, music, and slightly scaled-down mayhem.”  No matter what, An Evening of Alton Brown will present Brown as you’ve never seen him — unless, of course, you’ve been invited to his house. 
Best known for creating and hosting groundbreaking television such as Good Eats, Iron Chef America, Cutthroat Kitchen, and Food Network Star, Brown has also built one of the most successful culinary touring franchises ever, performing live in more than 300 cities for well over half a million fans. With An Evening of Alton Brown, he strips away the spectacle in favor of connection, spontaneity, and storytelling, delivering a show that is equal parts humor, science, food, and heart.

title: Nitty Gritty Dirt Band - All the Good Times: The Farewell Tour - 60 Years of Dirt
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
Nitty Gritty Dirt Band has been delighting audiences with their signature blend of country, folk, rock, and roots music. Widely regarded as one of the most influential bands in American roots history, their career spans multiple generations and includes timeless hits like “Mr. Bojangles,” “Fishin’ in the Dark,” “An American Dream,” and “House at Pooh Corner.” 
The All the Good Times Tour celebrates the band’s incredible legacy — from their groundbreaking Will the Circle Be Unbroken collaborations to their chart-topping radio favorites. With their trademark harmonies, masterful musicianship, and heartfelt storytelling, The Nitty Gritty Dirt Band continues to prove that great music only gets better with age. 
Joining NGDB is emerging country artist Meels with her soulful voice, heartfelt lyrics, and modern country flair. Blending authentic storytelling with infectious melodies, Meels is quickly capturing hearts and carving out her place in today’s country music scene.

description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Lancaster Live & Learn Series 2026 - The William Whipper Reading Circle
location: Lancaster, LPL Board Room
description:
Community, Discussion, Adults, Seniors

The Commonwealth Monument Project returns to the Lancaster Public Library for its annual LIVE AND LEARN CHAUTAUQUA.Presented in memory of the life and legacy of Lenwood Sloan, the 2026 series is a salute to PA Abolitionist William Whipper's galvanizing Reading Society. Five famous speeches presented by colleagues of Whipper are matched to five contemporary books selected by the library staff. ➡️ Read the book prior to the meeting, then we'll listen to the corresponding speech and discuss both during our gathering.Registration is helpful, but walk-ins will not be turned away.List of books:January 13 - Black Genius: Essays on an American Legacy by Tre JohnsonFebruary 10 - The New Jim Crow: Mass Incarceration in the Age of Colorblindness by Michelle Alexander March 10 - Misbehaving at the Crossroads by Honoree Fanonne Jeffers April 14 - Between the World and Me by Ta-Nehisi Coates May 12 - Citizen: An American Lyric by Claudia Rankine List of speeches:January 13 - David Walker, 1829 - Appeal to the Colored Citizens of the World!February 10 - William Whipper, 1834 - The Slavery of IntemperanceMarch 10 - Henry Highland Garnett, 1843 - Call to RebellionApril 14 - Thomas Morris Chester, 1862 - Negro Self Respect and Pride of RaceMay 12 - Frederick Douglass, 1894 - Lessons of the Hour

location: Lititz, LTZ Storytime Room

title: Family Place (Birth-3yo)
location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:
Community, Health & Wellness, Babies, Toddlers

Come play with us at Quarryville Library for our certified Family Place program! Two programs each week, differentiated by child’s age. If you have children in both groups, you may participate in both or choose a different one each week to get the maximum benefit!10 am-11 am –  Parent-child workshop (birth to 3 with caregiver) -providing a welcoming community environment with resources that families need to nurture their children’s development to help ensure they enter school ready and able to learn.Weekly topics:March 18: Early Literacy with Quarryville Library Youth staff plus FREE vision care screenings courtesy of Vision CorpsMarch 25: Speech and Language Development with Lancaster County Intervention April 1: Growth and Development with Thrive to Five / Parents as TeachersApril 8: Pediatric Nutrition with Penn State ExtensionApril 15: Music and Movement plus "Touch-a-Truck" ambulance and Family Safety with Wakefield EMSEach week offers opportunities for children to play with developmentally appropriate toys in a play group setting and for parents to meet and question community experts in the areas of child development, speech and language, nutrition, play, movement, and music.Learn more about what it means to be a Family Place library on our website!

title: Mom's Coffee Hour!
location: Manheim Community, MAN Meeting Room
description:
Business & Career, Community, Discussion, Adults

Hey moms! Join us for coffee and conversation on the third Wednesday of each month, 10-11 am. All ideas are welcome! This session is meant for moms to have a break and connect with other moms. Babes in arms are welcome. (We are not able to offer babysitting at this time.)

title: Morning Mahjong
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center C
description:
Community, Gaming, Adults, Seniors

🀄️✨Join us for a morning of strategy, skill, and socializing! 🀄️✨Whether you are a seasoned pro or a newcomer looking to practice, come enjoy the classic game of tiles in a friendly, community-led atmosphere, every Wednesday from 10-1 in Morgan Center C.Note: This is a self-directed community group. While no formal instructor is provided, players of all levels are welcome to learn from one another. What to Bring: Sets and racks (at least one per four players)Your current 2026 National Mah Jongg League cardA friendly, patient attitude! Community GuidelinesBe Welcoming:  We all started as beginners! Please be patient with those still learning to playThe "Rule of Five": If a fifth player joins your table, we suggest the "East Sits Out" rotation—the player in the East position rotates out for one hand so everyone gets to playFair Play:  Tables are available on a first-come, first-served basisPlease keep talk inclusive and the atmosphere kindRegistration is Required for this event

title: Wiggles and Giggles Ages 18 Months up to Age 4
location: Strasburg-Heisler
description:
Toddlers, Children

This Program is for Children ages 18 months up to age 4 with a caregiver Come sing, dance, Wiggle and Giggle! Miss Wendy's FUN monthly themes introduce basic concepts to toddlers and young children. Come play and learn with your little ones!! This class is a Library favorite!!Cancellations: This is a very popular class, so please cancel on the website or call the library if you are unable to attend the class that you are registered for. We typically have a waiting list and your cancellation allows others to attend.  3 "No Shows" and you will no longer be able to register for the program. Please reach out to us if you cannot attend or are running late. Thank you!

title: Toddler Time
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Toddlers

A time for Toddlers with their grown-up, dedicated to stories, discovery and exploring!  (18 months to 36 months, with adult. Other family members welcome.)Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 1st for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, April 3rd for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.

After reading a few stories we will enjoy lots of peer playtime! There will also be sessions with additional presenters to provide families with further education on their child and encouragement for partnership in the community through Family Place. Ages 0-2.

title: Play & Learn (3 - 5 Years)
location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:

Enjoy the play centers we have set out, along with a story and craft - just enough structure to get your child some practice with group learning but with caregivers at their side for support! We will also have early intervention experts on the side each week to provide information in case you have questions about your child's development. For ages 3-6 with parent or caregiver.Weekly topics:March 18: Early Literacy with Quarryville Library Youth staff plus FREE vision care screenings courtesy of Vision CorpsMarch 25: Speech and Language Development with Lancaster County Intervention April 1: Growth and Development with Thrive to Five / Parents as TeachersApril 8: Pediatric Nutrition with Penn State ExtensionApril 15: Music and Movement plus "Touch-a-Truck" ambulance and Family Safety with Wakefield EMS

title: Preschool Party
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Children

Join Ms. Laura for a fun and engaging preschool program designed to spark curiosity and a love for learning in young children! This program features stories, songs, and hands-on activities tailored for preschool-aged kids. Each session focuses on building early literacy, motor skills, and social interaction through playful exploration.Perfect for ages 3–6 with an adult, this program provides a welcoming and inclusive environment where kids can learn and grow together. Parents and caregivers are encouraged to participate and share in the joy of discovery with their little ones.Ms. Laura has over 20 years of preschool classroom experience bringing a wealth of knowledge in early childhood development. Her approach focuses on creating a warm, engaging, and supportive environment where children can thrive.Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 1st for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, April 3rd for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.Preschool Party Registration Requirements – All children who will be participating in the craft will need to be registered as a participant to allow for adequate supplies.

title: Storytime for Ages 3-5
location: Strasburg-Heisler
description:
Storytime, Children

Reading to young children is an important part of early child development and preparation for school. In Library Storytime, children are introduced to weekly themes and concept through books, rhymes, songs, and puppets. We also practice     important Preschool skills like taking turns, being active listeners, and learning to be part of in a group. **PLEASE be sure to include your email address on the registration. If you do not, you WILL NOT receive registration confirmation or notification if the event is cancelled.  We are unable to send you that information if you only include a phone number. -Thank You!

title: Wee Build Free Play
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
STEM, Toddlers, Children

Drop-in program. No registration required.Let your child’s imagination soar in this hands-on free play program! Designed for kids ages 2-7, this engaging session provides a creative and interactive environment where children can explore, build, and learn through play. No registration is required.Using vibrant magnetic tiles and a variety of manipulatives such as blocks, gears, and more, children will:Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills.Enhance fine motor abilities and hand-eye coordination.Foster collaboration and social interaction with peers.This open-ended program encourages creativity, exploration, and discovery, all while having fun! Whether constructing towering structures, creating intricate patterns, or experimenting with new ideas, kids will enjoy a safe, educational experience.Each family group must include a responsible adult in the room at all times. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

Guests will explore bright, sunny flavors and techniques inspired by Latin and island cuisines while learning how to balance sweet, savory, and citrus elements. This class focuses on working with fresh ingredients, building layered flavors, and creating vibrant dishes that feel both playful and approachable. 
Hands on. BYOB. Ages 16 and up.  Gluten Free

Bright Pineapple Salsa with Citrus & Fresh Herbs

Instead, give yourself permission to play, make mistakes, have fun, and just make bad art.THANK YOU for registering -- it will help us determine how many supplies we need! · Ages 18+Monthly Projects:January: A game of Pictionary (to celebrate the first club meet-up)February: Collage Night: Our Inner GardenMarch: Paint and Swap Night    April: Found PoetryMay: Play with Clay DoughJune: Blind Contour Art ChallengeJuly: Paint Night (Randomizer Challenge)August: Collage Night: Play September: Judge a Book by Its CoverOctober: Exquisite CorpseNovember: PictionaryDecember: DIY Card Deck (affirmation cards, oracle cards, etc.)

title: Trivia Night at Southern Market - Meet People Lancaster team
description:
Meet People Lancaster
Do you love trivia nights, but want to avoid the hassle of trying to wrangle up enough friends to field a team? Come play for a Meet People Lancaster team and make some new friends along the way!
Cost: FREE
Bar & food stations are all walk up. You’ll be on your own tab for the night.
Host: Mark Aumen
Please try to get there by 6:30PM, so we can get situated at a couple tables. Trivia starts at 7PM. If you come up early you’ll have time to mingle and get some food before trivia starts.
Location: 100 S Queen St, Lancaster, PA 17603
How to find the group: We have a couple acrylic sign holders on tables with Meet People Lancaster logos in them. We’ll also post an Instagram story with a picture of where your host, Mark, is sitting in the food court. Please follow Instagram stories for event updates. Not just for this event, but for all events.

title: The Sensory Social
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Arts & Crafts, Community, Adults

Looking for a place to relax, tinker, and meet new people? The Sensory Social is a low-stress hangout for neurodivergent adults. We’ve gathered a variety of tactile and creative stations for you to explore at your own pace.Enjoy our sequin wall, motion bubblers, and fidget kits, or try your hand at the knit board and art stations. This is a self-guided, 'choose your own adventure' space where you can be yourself among peers. If you need a hand using the materials, please bring a friend or support person along. Come hang out and find what feels good!This program is designed for adult folks with IDD & autism. We want everyone to participate with their peers, regardless of motor or communication skills.  Please do not remove supplies from the room.

description:

title: Spotting the Fake

description:

title: Mocktails & Meaning™: Mental Health in Young Men
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
April’s Mocktails & Meaning™ invites you into an honest and important conversation with Ben Faro from the Samaritan Center as we focus on Mental Health in Young Men. In a world that often teaches boys to stay silent about their struggles, this session will explore the pressures young men face, the warning signs we should not ignore, and the ways families, churches, and communities can foster openness, resilience, and hope. We’ll gather over a festive Easter Punch mocktail while creating space for thoughtful dialogue and meaningful questions. Come ready for a conversation that encourages courage, compassion, and connection.

title: The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $34 $44 $54 
The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA Ticket + Merch Bundle  $139 
      Package Includes: 

Great Ticket to the show
Limited Edition, The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA Poster
The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA Exclusive Merchandise Item

Merchandise items are shipped the week of the show date and may arrive shortly before or after the show. Questions regarding the VIP package can be directed to VIP@ONELIVE.COM. 
For well over a decade, The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA has continued to be the top ABBA tribute group in the world, dazzling all who see with their fantastic performance while playing the most iconic hits from ABBA, including “Mamma Mia,” “S.O.S,” “Money, Money, Money,” “The Winner Takes All,” “Waterloo,” “Gimme, Gimme, Gimme,” and “Dancing Queen.”  The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA brings one of the greatest pop phenomena back to life.  Audiences and critics agree, The Concert: A Tribute to ABBA is the most amazing and authentic ABBA tribute show in the world – “This is the closest to ABBA you’ll ever get.”  Come dance, come sing, having the time of your life at THE ULTIMATE TRIBUTE CELEBRATION! 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Block Party!
location: Quarryville, QVL Story Corner/Early Literacy Lab
description:
Community, STEM, Toddlers, Children

Registration is requested, drop ins are accommodated as space allows. Please join the waitlist as we may schedule a second session if there is sufficient demand.Block Party is an hour long free play session for preschoolers and their caregivers using our library's wooden block set, people, vehicles, animals, scarves, photographs of buildings, and other accompanying materials where preschoolers have the freedom to build and play as they wish.An adult staff member or volunteer is present to model open ended questions and other supports for the adults participating.Young children learn best when they are able to construct knowledge through meaningful play. With the materials provided children will build and experiment, manipulate objects, work with others, count and measure, talk about what they are building and building vocabulary; all activities that will help children to achieve the Pennsylvania Learning Standards for early childhood in not only STEM areas, but also in language and literacy development, physical development and social and emotional development.Block party has only three rules:1) Adult caregiver must be present in room2) No throwing blocks3) No knocking down anyone else's buildingThis program is sponsored by Reynolds & Shivery Funeral Home.

description:
Arts & Crafts, Children, Tweens

We will celebrate the life and times of Courtney Moore. Courtney is the best gamer at the arcade. But she can’t understand why there aren’t more girl characters in the games she plays. When a school project allows her to create her own video game, the hero is a girl who knows how to handle any situation—something Courtney struggles with in real life. Whether it’s learning to share a room with her stepsister or supporting her mom when she announces she’s running for mayor, Courtney’s blended family has to learn to work together differently. It’s a whole new game for Courtney, and she’s figuring out the rules as she goes. We’ll have tea, snacks and a craft from that time period. Come in costume if you wish and bring your doll! Ages 8-12.

title: America250 in Manheim
location: Manheim Community, MAN Kreider Cultural Center
description:
Arts & Crafts, Community, Discussion, Fundraiser, Music, Presentation, Adults, Seniors

To Purchase your Tickets, Use This Evite Link: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1982453951134?aff=oddtdtcreatorGet ready to dive into the flavors of American history with A Taste of History-America250 in Manheim Fundraiser - a delicious journey awaitsOn Saturday, April 18th, the Manheim Historical Society and Manheim Community Library invite you to join them as we take a look back in time and toast five American patriots who have significant connections to Manheim. With each wine tasting experience offered by Glasshouse Wineworks of Manheim, a unique wine is made for each patriot, and each person's story is told. Desserts and hors d'oeuvres associated with the era will also be served. Visitors may sit and catch up with friends, make new acquaintances, and savor a cash bar of wine and beer as they walk from wine station to wine station. We appreciate all proceeds, which will help the Manheim Community Library and Manheim Historical Society maintain our local history while promoting reading and lifelong learning.What's included for adults over 21:General Admission pay $75 for a standard ticket that includes desserts, hors d'oeuvres, and wine tastings. Cash bar of wine and beer available.VIP tickets are $95 and include everything described above, as well as a personalized wine glass and one glass of your favorite wine.Tickets must be pre-purchased online or by mailing checks made payable to Manheim Historical Society to Box 396, Manheim, PA 17545. Kindly specify the type and quantity of tickets. Tickets must be purchased in advance, by April 10th.The Kreider Farms Cultural Center will open its doors for check-in at 5:45 p.m. At 6:15 p.m., wine- tasting tables will open. Bottles of wine will be available for purchase at the event but will be held until end of event.

title: Folsom Prison Experience - A Johnny Cash Music/Dramatic Production
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $24 $34 $44 
Prepare to step back in time and immerse yourself in the unforgettable atmosphere of the legendary 1968 Johnny Cash show at Folsom Prison, where the spirit of that historic day springs to life. 
As the doors swing open, you’ll find yourself instantly transformed into an inmate, ready to embark on a captivating firsthand experience. Marvel at the commanding presence of Johnny Cash himself, joined by the incomparable June Carter and the melodious harmonies of the Statler Brothers. Sense the palpable tension as the watchful warden and his guards maintain order within the prison walls. 
Leading you through this extraordinary journey is the ever-serious emcee, Hugh Cherry. Get ready to be transported, as you become an integral part of this immersive tribute, capturing the raw energy and soul that made the Folsom Prison performance truly remarkable. A memorable show for all ages. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

Project Linus welcomes blankets in all styles, including quilts, tied comforters, fleece blankets, crocheted and knitted afghans. No registration needed. description:

title: Google Suite: Sheets (Intermediate)
location: Ephrata, EPH Conference Room
description:
Technology Class, Adults, Seniors

Participants will be able to understand and use the sorting data functions, auto sum functions, absolute cell references and auto fill. Participants will be able to describe and implement the order of operations in mathematics to be able to accurately write formulas. This class will focus on intermediate skills to manage finances, mailing lists and other types of spreadsheets. Participants should already have a Google account. This class is supported by a grant from the Members 1st Charitable Foundation. Ages 18+.

title: "The Librarians" Film Screening
location: Ephrata, EPH Multipurpose Room
description:
Movie & Film, Teens, Adults, Seniors

Across the U.S., librarians face the impact of uniting against library collection standards that include restrictions on race-related and LGBTQIA+ content. Drawing on historical context, “The Librarians” explores the broader implications for education and public life. Join us for an in-depth look at the fight against libraries and how book review policies are shaping their communities. Ages 16+.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Save the Pollinators!
location: Milanof-Schock
description:

Celebrate Earth Day by learning about the tiny heroes that help our plants grow! Kids will explore the world of bees, butterflies, and other pollinators through games, crafts, and interactive activities. We will discover how pollinators make our gardens bloom and how we can help protect them! We will even plant some seeds of our own so we can start helping the pollinators do their job!

title: Make Calls with Turn PA Blue for the 2026 Primary Election!

description: ### Join Turn PA Blue as we make calls for the 2026 Primary Election! We'll be making calls using the Scale to Win Auto-Dialer, a computer program that dials FOR YOU! At the start of the shift, we will review the script and provide dialer training to anyone who needs it. Then we'll make calls together until the end of the shift.*

What to Bring: Sets and racks (at least one per four players)Your current 2026 National Mah Jongg League cardA friendly, patient attitude! Community GuidelinesBe Welcoming:  We all started as beginners! Please be patient with those still learning to playThe "Rule of Five": If a fifth player joins your table, we suggest the "East Sits Out" rotation—the player in the East position rotates out for one hand so everyone gets to playFair Play:  Tables are available on a first-come, first-served basisPlease keep talk inclusive and the atmosphere kindRegistration is Required for this event

title: One Night of Queen performed by Gary Mullen & The Works
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, September 10 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, September 12 at 10:00 a.m. 
For over 20+ years Gary Mullen and The Works have been performing their world renowned One Night of Queen live concert.  The band will have you dancing in the aisles during their show while they pay tribute to the stage theatrics, showmanship, and music of Rock & Roll Hall of Fame inductees, Queen.  Gary Mullen & The Works have performed to sold-out audiences around the world, playing more than 2,500 live shows to audiences in over 20 countries – performing in many of the same venues that Queen have played at around the world.  The Show DOES go on! 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
For more than 30 years, Grammy Award-winning country band Shenandoah has delivered heartfelt songs and rich harmonies that helped shape the sound of modern country music. Led by original frontman Marty Raybon, the group rose to fame with beloved hits like “Two Dozen Roses,” “Church on Cumberland Road,” “Next to You, Next to Me,” and “I Want to Be Loved Like That.” 
Known for their genuine lyrics and timeless sound, Shenandoah continues to captivate audiences with a mix of nostalgia and new energy. Experience an evening filled with classic favorites and the unmistakable charm of one of country music’s most enduring groups.

location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $29 $34 $39 
Stayin’ Alive offers to their audiences the songs and sights of a full Bee Gees play list, singing blockbusters such as “Night Fever,” “Jive Talkin’,” “How Deep Is Your Love,” “You Should Be Dancing,” “Nights on Broadway,” and “Stayin’ Alive.” In addition, they perform softer poetic ballads such as “I Started a Joke,” “Massachusetts,” “Fanny Be Tender,” “Words,” and “To Love Somebody” among other great hits. Stayin’ Alive is the largest and most definitive production of its kind, offering big screen video clips, photos, and dazzling imagery. Stayin’ Alive is the quintessential tribute band to the Bee Gees, capturing the excitement of live performance and the tender subtleties of the human voice. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: My Generation Tour: The Lovin Spoonful, Terry Sylvester and Gary Lewis & The Playboys
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $79 $89
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, October 1 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, October 3 at 10:00 a.m. 
“This good-time spectacular celebrates the time of our lives with all three acts performing with their own full bands spanning the golden singles-era of pop music, from 1965 to 1975. This was the magic time where hit singles ruled the charts and won our hearts,” producer Jim Della Croce exclaims. Terry Sylvester, who famously replaced Graham Nash after he left The Hollies, will perform along with famed pop legend Gary Lewis. “This show is the baby-boomer bucket list must see event of the year, a hip trip back to the era that defined our generation,” he concludes. 
Between The Lovin’ Spoonful, The Hollies, and Gary Lewis and The Playboys, the charts exploded every week with a new tune that captivated our rock and roll souls. Nearly 30 hits will explode throughout this two-hour celebration: “Daydream,” “Do You Believe in Magic,” “This Diamond Ring,” “Carrie Anne,” “Long Cool Woman (in a Black Dress), “Count Me In,” “He Ain’t Heavy, He’s My Brother,” “Summer in the City,” “She’s Just My Style,” “You Didn’t Have to Be So Nice,” “On a Carousel,”  “Sure Gonna Miss Her” and “The Air that I Breathe.” 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. title: Asking the Hard Questions
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
At Church of the Apostles, we know many are carrying deep and honest questions about faith, especially in light of what is happening in our country and throughout the world. Questions like “Where is God in all of this?” are not signs of weak faith, but part of a faithful journey we are called to walk together. 
To create space for these conversations, we invite you to a new monthly series, Asking the Hard Questions, hosted by our seminary intern, Deklan Lewis, in the Parlor. Together, we will explore doubt, struggle, and belief as meaningful parts of discipleship. Our first two gatherings will include viewing and discussing the film God on Trial as we wrestle, much like Jacob, with faith and what it means to follow Jesus today. 
To accommodate different schedules, two sessions will be offered each month: Monday morning, April 27th at 10 AM, and Thursday evening, April 30th at 6 PM. All are welcome to join this thoughtful, open, and supportive conversation.

title: Teen Book Talk: A Little Princess
location: Manheim Township, MTP Study Room #119
description:
Book Club, Teens

For Teens ages 13-18 years of age only.Calling all teen readers! Join us for Teen Book Talk, a fun and engaging monthly book discussion group led by members of our Teen Advisory Board! Book selections are chosen by teens during advisory meetings. Interested in joining the Teen Advisory Board? Sign up through the library calendar. Each month, we’ll dive into a new title—ranging from trending young adult fiction to graphic novels, fantasy epics, thought-provoking contemporary stories, and more.This month we'll be reading "A Little Princess" by Frances Hodgson. Sara Crewe looks like a princess as she enters boarding school - she wears beautiful clothes and has the best manners.  But what happens when all of the money disappears and Sara is left with nothing?  She is forced to sleep in the attic of the school and is wearing rags.  Can Sara overcome this "riches to rags" turn of events and come out as the princess she knows she is in the end? You’ll get the chance to share your thoughts, hear different perspectives, and connect with fellow book lovers in a relaxed and welcoming space. Snacks will be provided, and occasionally we’ll have themed activities or giveaways tied to the book!Note: To help everyone stay present and get the most out of the experience, we ask that all phones stay in pockets or bags during the program. Thank you for your cooperation!Library Program PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Preschool Party
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Children

Join Ms. Laura for a fun and engaging preschool program designed to spark curiosity and a love for learning in young children! This program features stories, songs, and hands-on activities tailored for preschool-aged kids. Each session focuses on building early literacy, motor skills, and social interaction through playful exploration.Perfect for ages 3–6 with an adult, this program provides a welcoming and inclusive environment where kids can learn and grow together. Parents and caregivers are encouraged to participate and share in the joy of discovery with their little ones.Ms. Laura has over 20 years of preschool classroom experience bringing a wealth of knowledge in early childhood development. Her approach focuses on creating a warm, engaging, and supportive environment where children can thrive.Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 15th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, April 17th for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.Preschool Party Registration Requirements – All children who will be participating in the craft will need to be registered as a participant to allow for adequate supplies.

title: Lit Lovers Book Group
location: Milanof-Schock, MJY Grissinger Board Room
description:
Book Club, Adults

Lit Lovers is an eclectic reading group with a primary focus on fiction, while also exploring memoirs, short stories, biographies and autobiographies, mysteries, science fiction, and select works of nonfiction. The group is known for its lively, thoughtful discussions and makes a concerted effort to respect differing viewpoints. To keep conversations focused and meaningful, social chatter, gossip, and romance novels are generally kept outside the allotted discussion time.Lit Lovers gravitate toward modern fiction distinguished by strong writing, well-developed characters, and compelling narratives. Common themes include contemporary issues, family conflict, and the triumph of the human spirit, with a healthy appreciation for humor. Members read for both personal enrichment and enjoyment, and the group frequently selects works by international authors, books set abroad, or stories that explore multicultural perspectives.For a list of this year's book selections, click on the photo to the left or pick up a bookmark at the library!

title: Morning Mahjong
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center C
description:

🀄️✨Join us for a morning of strategy, skill, and socializing! 🀄️✨Whether you are a seasoned pro or a newcomer looking to practice, come enjoy the classic game of tiles in a friendly, community-led atmosphere, every Wednesday from 10-1 in Morgan Center C.Note: This is a self-directed community group. While no formal instructor is provided, players of all levels are welcome to learn from one another. What to Bring: Sets and racks (at least one per four players)Your current 2026 National Mah Jongg League cardA friendly, patient attitude! Community GuidelinesBe Welcoming:  We all started as beginners! Please be patient with those still learning to playThe "Rule of Five": If a fifth player joins your table, we suggest the "East Sits Out" rotation—the player in the East position rotates out for one hand so everyone gets to playFair Play:  Tables are available on a first-come, first-served basisPlease keep talk inclusive and the atmosphere kindRegistration is Required for this event

title: Trivia Night at Southern Market - Meet People Lancaster team
description:

Cost: FREE
Bar & food stations are all walk up. You’ll be on your own tab for the night.
Host: Mark Aumen
Please try to get there by 6:30PM, so we can get situated at a couple tables. Trivia starts at 7PM. If you come up early you’ll have time to mingle and get some food before trivia starts.
Location: 100 S Queen St, Lancaster, PA 17603
How to find the group: We have a couple acrylic sign holders on tables with Meet People Lancaster logos in them. We’ll also post an Instagram story with a picture of where your host, Mark, is sitting in the food court. Please follow Instagram stories for event updates. Not just for this event, but for all events.

title: The Sensory Social
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Arts & Crafts, Community, Adults

Looking for a place to relax, tinker, and meet new people? The Sensory Social is a low-stress hangout for neurodivergent adults. We’ve gathered a variety of tactile and creative stations for you to explore at your own pace.Enjoy our sequin wall, motion bubblers, and fidget kits, or try your hand at the knit board and art stations. This is a self-guided, 'choose your own adventure' space where you can be yourself among peers. If you need a hand using the materials, please bring a friend or support person along. Come hang out and find what feels good!This program is designed for adult folks with IDD & autism. We want everyone to participate with their peers, regardless of motor or communication skills.  Please do not remove supplies from the room.

title: Asking the Hard Questions
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
At Church of the Apostles, we know many are carrying deep and honest questions about faith, especially in light of what is happening in our country and throughout the world. Questions like “Where is God in all of this?” are not signs of weak faith, but part of a faithful journey we are called to walk together. 
To create space for these conversations, we invite you to a new monthly series, Asking the Hard Questions, hosted by our seminary intern, Deklan Lewis, in the Parlor. Together, we will explore doubt, struggle, and belief as meaningful parts of discipleship. Our first two gatherings will include viewing and discussing the film God on Trial as we wrestle, much like Jacob, with faith and what it means to follow Jesus today. 
To accommodate different schedules, two sessions will be offered each month: Monday morning, April 27th at 10 AM, and Thursday evening, April 30th at 6 PM. All are welcome to join this thoughtful, open, and supportive conversation.

title: Leslie Odom, Jr.
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $79 $99 $109 $129
 
Leslie Odom, Jr. is a Tony and Grammy Award-winning, three-time Emmy and two-time Academy Award-nominated songwriter, actor, and a New York Times bestselling author. Well known for his breakout role as ‘Aaron Burr’ in the Pulitzer Prize-winning Broadway musical Hamilton, Odom made the season’s most highly anticipated return to his award-winning role in September 2025. During his limited engagement run, which came nine years after his final bow, Hamilton hit its highest ever gross in its ten years on Broadway. 
Odom’s long-awaited return to Broadway following his original Hamilton run came in 2023 as a producer, alongside his beloved Nicolette Robinson and the gracious Jeffrey Richards, with the classic American comedy Purlie Victorious: A Non-Confederate Romp Through the Cotton Patch written by the legendary Ossie Davis. The revival opened to widespread critical acclaim. Odom was Tony-nominated for his performance, and the production received six nominations and one win. Purlie Victorious was recorded and aired as part of PBS’ Great Performances.  
Odom has continued to captivate audiences across film and television including his star turn as legendary soul singer Sam Cooke in the Amazon film adaptation of One Night in Miami…, directed by Regina King. His lauded portrayal of the soul icon and musical performance of original song “Speak Now” earned him multiple awards and nominations. Additional film and television credits include Rian Johnson’s record-breaking 2022 Knives Out sequel, Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery, for Netflix, The Many Saints of Newark, a prequel to David Chase’s award-winning HBO series The Sopranos, Apple TV+’s Central Park (2020 Emmy nomination), Hamilton on Disney+ (2021 Emmy nomination), host of “The Tony Awards Present: Broadway’s Back!” on CBS (2022 Emmy nomination), Abbott Elementary, The Proud Family: Louder and Prouder, Harriet, and many more. 
n March 2018, Odom added the title of author to his resume with the release of his book – Failing Up: How to Take Risks, Aim Higher and Never Stop Learning, which was recently released in paperback in March 2025. Co-written with Nicolette Robinson, Odom’s first children’s book, I Love You More Than You’ll Ever Know, was published by Feiwel & Friends in March 2023. The book debuted on the New York Times bestseller list at #7 in its first week. Odom has released six full-length albums. His sixth and first-ever live album, An Offering: Live at Speakeasy Studios, was released on October 17, 2025. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Grand Opening Weekend of the Stevens & Smith Center
location: The Stevens & Smith Center, 13-15 East Vine Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
It’s the moment we’ve all been waiting for: the Grand Opening of the Thaddeus Stevens & Lydia Hamilton Smith Center for History and Democracy! 
Join LancasterHistory for a weekend celebration, including a ribbon cutting, Grand Opening Gala, and a family-friendly community block party! 
More information to come!

title: STOMP
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $44 $54 $64 $84 
STOMP is explosive, provocative, sophisticated, sexy, utterly unique, and appeals to audiences of all ages.  The international percussion sensation has garnered an armful of awards and rave reviews and has appeared on numerous national television shows. The eight-member troupe uses everything but conventional percussion instruments – matchboxes, wooden poles, brooms, garbage cans, Zippo lighters, hubcaps – to fill the stage with magnificent rhythms.  The return of the percussive hit also brings some new surprises, with some sections of the show now updated and restructured and the addition of two new full-scale routines, utilizing props like tractor tire inner tubes and paint cans.  As USA Today says, “STOMP finds beautiful noises in the strangest places.”  STOMP.  See what all the noise is about. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Grand Opening Ribbon Cutting
location: The Stevens & Smith Center, 13-15 East Vine Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Opening on May 1, 2026, in the heart of downtown Lancaster, the restored historic properties once owned by Thaddeus Stevens and his partner Lydia Hamilton Smith are re-envisioned as a state-of-the-art museum and education space. Innovative exhibits, created with leading historians and designers, confront slavery, freedom, and the ongoing fight for equality in America. LancasterHistory—together with many collaborative partners—are eager to welcome the public for this once-in-a-generation moment for our community, a celebration as bold as the history it honors!

The excitement kicks off Friday morning as we officially open the Stevens & Smith Center with a celebratory ribbon-cutting! Doors open at 8am for coffee, light fare, and mingling. At 9am, the program begins with inspiring remarks and special guest performances that set the stage for a powerful new chapter.

This event is free and open to the entire community; we can’t wait to celebrate with you! Please RSVP so we can ensure ample seating and plenty of breakfast items for everyone. Join us as we mark this historic moment together and open the doors to something extraordinary!

Tickets coming soon!

description:

title: Mako & Haejin Fujimura
location: The Trust Performing Arts Center, 37 N. Market St., Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Sponsored by The Open Discourse Coalition, this special evening at The Trust welcomes back Makoto Fujimura, accompanied by his wife, Haejin Fujimura, for a conversation centered on their forthcoming book, “Beauty X Justice.” 
We often imagine beauty and justice as opposites: one concerned with truth and fairness, the other with aesthetics and adornment. What if they aren’t rivals at all? What if beauty and justice belong together, moving in step to create lives, communities, and cultures marked by courage and abundance? 
In this fireside-style conversation, the Fujimuras will explore how beauty and justice can work hand in hand, drawing from their shared life, creative practice, and public work. More than ideas alone, this evening offers a close look at how their vision takes shape in the real world, through art, advocacy, and cultural renewal. 
The conversation will be guided by The Trust’s Founder & Curator, Tom Becker, beneath the Beaux Arts vaulted ceiling of The Trust Performing Arts Center in downtown Lancaster. 
Bring your friends, your curiosity, and your questions. Seating is free with advanced registration. Space is limited, so reserve your tickets early! 
Reserve Your Tickets

title: Chickies Rock Warbler Walk
location: Chickies Rock Breezy View Overlook (Meeting Location), 770 Breezyview Dr., Columbia, PA, 17512, United States
description:
Marie Johnston will lead a field trip to Chickies Rock County Park. Meet at 7 a.m. at Breezy View Overlook along Rt. 441 just north of Columbia. After birding Breezy View, which includes a view of a Bald Eagle’s nest on the York County side of the Susquehanna River, the group will proceed to the Chickies Rock Overlook parking lot and bird in the park on both sides of Rt. 441. The field trip will end in the day-use area of the park. In addition to warblers, target species include orioles, tanagers, and flycatchers. Be prepared for ticks and bring water and snacks/lunch. Contact Marie (717-413-2086 or mariejohnston303@gmail.com) if you have questions or by 6 p.m. on the previous day if you plan to attend. 
Meeting Location: Breezy View Overlook near 770 Breezyview Dr., Columbia, PA 17512

The morning will be spent looking for migrants and resident birds along this popular stretch of Hammer Creek. A wide variety of birds are possible with special attention paid to the warblers that use this area as their home or a brief stopover during migration. Some other target birds will include thrushes, flycatchers, tanagers, orioles and grosbeaks. This trip will last until around noontime so dress for the weather. Please contact Carl at 717-471-9969 or fledge19@verizon.net by 6 p.m. the previous day if you plan to attend.

description:
Make plans for a special Mocktails & Meaning™ gathering on Saturday, May 2nd at 2:30 PM in the Sanctuary as we host a screening of the documentary The Librarians. 
This powerful film follows librarians across the United States who find themselves on the front lines of debates about censorship, intellectual freedom, and access to information. Through their stories, the documentary explores what it means to defend the freedom to read in a time when books, ideas, and education are increasingly challenged in schools and communities. 
Following the screening, we will hold a Q&A and open discussion, creating space to reflect on the themes of the film and share thoughts together as a community. All are welcome for this thoughtful afternoon of film, conversation, and connection.

HarrowAugust 4: “The Sniper” by Liam O’FlahertySeptember 1: “How to Pronounce Knife,” “Chick-A-Chee,” and “Edge of the World” by Souvankham ThammavongsaOctober 6: “The Summer People” by Shirley JacksonNovember 3: “The Lottery Ticket” by Anton Chekhov and “Contents of the Dead Man’s Pocket” by Jack FinneyDecember 1: “The Burglar’s Christmas” by Willa Cather (under the pseudonym “Elizabeth L. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

title: Financial Wellness Overview (virtual meeting)
location: Lancaster

This online session is facilitated by two experienced female financial advisors who are passionate about empowering individuals to take control of their finances. The seminar will give you the tools you need to make informed financial decisions.Our hosts will share their expertise about practical, real-life financial topics - from saving, investing, to retirement planning, social security, as well as life insurance and disability income insurance.Thank you for registering.➡️ We will be using Zoom to host this program. You will need either a computer with a microphone and webcam, or a smartphone to connect. After you register, you will receive the meeting link via email.Sheila Stotler & Tara Hauber are registered representatives of and offers securities, investment advisory and financial planning services through MML Investors Services, LLC. We’ll also post an Instagram story with a picture of where your host, Mark, is sitting in the food court. Please follow Instagram stories for event updates. description: We love democracy. We love crafts. We love snacks. And we love civic engagement. You too? title: Leonid & Friends
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $44 $54 $64 $74
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, September 24, 2025 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, September 26, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. 
With multiple sold-out U.S. tours under its belt and hundreds of thousands of fans around the world, Leonid & Friends continues to astound its global audience with its unique ability in capturing the spirit, musicality, and fire of American supergroup Chicago. 
And what is even more stunning is that Leonid & Friends, comprised of 11 of the finest musicians in Russia, haven’t seen Chicago perform live until recently, yet replicates the band’s complex arrangements amazingly note for note. Chicago has never been in Russia, and none of us have attended their concerts until just recently in the United States,” said Leonid Vorobyev, a multi-instrumentalist who is the band’s leader. “We have only recordings and videos (to help us learn Chicago songs).” 
Ironically, Vorobyev wasn’t always a fan of horn bands, and as a teenager gravitated to the hard-driving guitar-based sounds of the Beatles, Creedence Clearwater Revival, Deep Purple, and Grand Funk Railroad. “In the early 1970s in the USSR, electric guitars, amplifiers, and drums were rare to find so at school we were playing self-made instruments,” Vorobyev explained. “We were allowed to rehearse in the same room with a horns band. Those musicians almost always were drunk and quite often tried to play with us our rock songs. It was a nightmare, and I hated them all!” 
But that all changed when Vorobyev heard Chicago. He became an instant fan. “I loved so much the brilliant mixture of rock guitars, drums, and jazz horns with bright vocal harmonies,” he said.
In 2014, Vorobyev decided to indulge his love for the band by gathering a few fellow musicians in a studio to record a video of them playing his favorite Chicago tune, Brand New Love Affair. No charts or scores of the song existed so Vorobyev transcribed the tune by ear. The result was a YouTube video that quickly went viral. Since then Leonid & Friends has recorded videos of other Chicago hits including Make Me Smile, 25 or 6 to 4, and Saturday In The Park. 
All total the videos have received more than 50 million views. Also, Leonid & Friends has recorded two albums showcasing its tribute to Chicago. In September of 2018, the band recorded its first song from legendary supergroup Earth, Wind, & Fire and also paid tribute to the band Blood, Sweat & Tears as well. 
Borrowing a line from one of Chicago’s biggest hits, “it’s only the beginning” of great things from Leonid & Friends.

title: Beginner Birder Trip to Flory Park
location: Flory Park (Meet at Clark Pavilion), 416 Dohner Rd., Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description: Sarah Gotwols will lead a morning spring migration beginner birder walk along Mill Creek at East Lampeter Township’s Flory Park. Meet the leader at 8 a.m. at the Clark Pavilion. This trip is for all ages and open to anyone who is new to birding or would like to polish their skills. Participants can expect to learn proper use of binoculars, field guides, and apps to help hone your bird watching skills. A variety of birds – to include resident and seasonal migrant species – should be expected on the trip. Please bring binoculars if you have them – a limited number of binoculars will also be available to borrow. Contact Sarah (717-940-3804 call/text or email sarahann71880@gmail.com) by 6 p.m. on Friday if you plan to attend. If necessary, weather-related cancellations will be made the day prior and registered participants will be notified, walk-ons are also permitted. This event is part of a partnership between Lancaster County Bird Club and East Lampeter Township Recreation. Learn more about Lancaster County Bird Club at www.lancasterbirdclub.org.

title: Herman's Hermits Starring Peter Noone with Chris Ruggiero
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $89 
Herman’s Hermits
British Invasion icon Peter Noone—the unmistakable voice of Herman’s Hermits—brings a hit-packed evening of songs, stories, and good-natured humor. Backed by his seasoned band, Noone revisits the soundtrack of the 1960s with favorites like “I’m Into Something Good,” “Mrs. Brown, You’ve Got a Lovely Daughter,” “There’s a Kind of Hush,” “I’m Henry VIII, I Am,” “Silhouettes,” and more. Expect crisp harmonies, jangly guitars, and the playful stage presence that’s endeared him to fans across generations. Between songs, Peter shares crowd-pleasing anecdotes from the road and the heyday of the British Invasion, turning the concert into an intimate, feel-good celebration of timeless pop. Whether you grew up with these classics or you’re discovering them for the first time, this sing-along night will leave you smiling—a joyful trip back to the golden age of ’60s pop with a performer who still makes it feel brand new.
 
Chris Ruggiero
Contemporary crooner Chris Ruggiero brings the spirit of the ’60s and ’70s to life in a shimmering, feel-good concert of timeless pop. Backed by a crack band and lush arrangements, Chris leans into the romance, rhythm, and melody of the era, delivering fresh takes that honor the originals while letting his smooth, soaring vocals shine. Sing along to favorites like “This Magic Moment,” “My Cherie Amour,” “More Today Than Yesterday,” “Unchained Melody,” “Can’t Take My Eyes Off You,” and more—interwoven with charming stories about the artists and the songs that shaped a generation. With easy charisma, crisp harmonies, and plenty of heart, Ruggiero turns nostalgia into a living, breathing celebration—perfect for date night, family night, or anyone who loves great songs sung beautifully. If you treasure classic pop and soul, this uplifting evening will leave you smiling long after the final note.

title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Oh Captain My Captain! for Tweens
location: Ephrata, EPH 1/2 Multipurpose Room Kitchen Side
description:
Gaming, Tweens

Oh Captain, My Captain! is a short form worldbuilding role-playing game (RPG) that explores heroism and the complicated relationships we have with people we admire. The game revolves around players drawing from a deck of cards with open-ended questions. Each question players answer helps them learn about their character, their world, their captain and the complicated relationship they have with a living legend. Players use these answers to make connections between their stories leading up one of three final questions that end the game. No experience or prep needed! Ages 10-12.

title: Lancaster Live & Learn Series 2026 - The William Whipper Reading Circle
location: Lancaster, LPL Board Room
description:
Community, Discussion, Adults, Seniors

The Commonwealth Monument Project returns to the Lancaster Public Library for its annual LIVE AND LEARN CHAUTAUQUA.Presented in memory of the life and legacy of Lenwood Sloan, the 2026 series is a salute to PA Abolitionist William Whipper's galvanizing Reading Society. Five famous speeches presented by colleagues of Whipper are matched to five contemporary books selected by the library staff. ➡️ Read the book prior to the meeting, then we'll listen to the corresponding speech and discuss both during our gathering.Registration is helpful, but walk-ins will not be turned away.List of books:January 13 - Black Genius: Essays on an American Legacy by Tre JohnsonFebruary 10 - The New Jim Crow: Mass Incarceration in the Age of Colorblindness by Michelle Alexander March 10 - Misbehaving at the Crossroads by Honoree Fanonne Jeffers April 14 - Between the World and Me by Ta-Nehisi Coates May 12 - Citizen: An American Lyric by Claudia Rankine List of speeches:January 13 - David Walker, 1829 - Appeal to the Colored Citizens of the World!February 10 - William Whipper, 1834 - The Slavery of IntemperanceMarch 10 - Henry Highland Garnett, 1843 - Call to RebellionApril 14 - Thomas Morris Chester, 1862 - Negro Self Respect and Pride of RaceMay 12 - Frederick Douglass, 1894 - Lessons of the Hour

title: Farm to Fork Experience
location: Bird-in-Hand Village Inn & Suites, 2695 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
Real Farms • Real Food
May 13, 2026
Food doesn’t get any more authentic than Bird-in-Hand’s Farm-to-Fork Experience. From milking cows to making ice cream to growing chemical-free produce from seed, you’ll meet Mennonite and Amish farmers and get an intimate view of how Lancaster County’s bounty begins. Our own John Smucker, an eighth-generation farmer, will be your guide on this unforgettable experience, which culminates in a mouth-watering farm-to-fork meal. 
The Farm to Fork Experience package includes:
• Two-night stay in the historic Bird-in-Hand Village Inn & Suites
• Tours of local farms and a lunch made with ingredients from our tour – see itinerary below
• Daily deluxe continental breakfasts for two at the Village Inn & Suites or credit at the Bird-in-Hand Bakery & Cafe
• Dinner for two at the Bird-in-Hand Family Restaurant & Smorgasbord on Tuesday or Wednesday
• Welcome market basket filled with local foods 
Space is limited so reserve early. Enjoy this package for $585 (plus tax). Extend your stay for only $109 a night (plus tax). For more information on the Village Inn & Suites, click here. Walking is required for these tours. 
Requires $200 deposit to reserve your tour (refundable up to two weeks prior to event). $109 extra night available for any room type. Taxes not included. 
  
2026 TOUR ITINERARY 
Rise, Shine & Dine (5:30 am): Wake up with the roosters and see cows being milked at our neighbor Aaron Lantz’s organic farm. Then savor a delicious farm-fresh breakfast with the Lantz family in their kitchen. 
Hydroponic Farming: At Millcreek Hydroponics, we’ll learn about the sustainable year-round growing methods for their lettuce and microgreens, and tour their greenhouse. 
The Time is Ripe: Take an orchard tour of Kauffman’s Fruit & Farm Market and learn how they produce their apple cider. 
Veggies on the Vine: Amish farmer Ephraim Huyard will show us his biological methods of growing plants, and how he grows the chemical-free vegetable plants. 
Growing Green: Meet Wesley Groff, third-generation farmer of Groff’s Celery and Vegetable Farm, and learn how he grows crops from seed in a chemical-free environment. 
Grown Local Luncheon: You’ve seen where it’s grown. Now taste the fresh difference. Receive a market basket of local foods after lunch. 
Save Room for Dessert: Our last stop after lunch will be a visit to the Bird-in-Hand Bakery & Cafe, where we make our artisan ice cream.
