title: Preschool Party
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Children

Join Ms. Laura for a fun and engaging preschool program designed to spark curiosity and a love for learning in young children! This program features stories, songs, and hands-on activities tailored for preschool-aged kids. Each session focuses on building early literacy, motor skills, and social interaction through playful exploration.Perfect for ages 3–6 with an adult, this program provides a welcoming and inclusive environment where kids can learn and grow together. Parents and caregivers are encouraged to participate and share in the joy of discovery with their little ones.Ms. Laura has over 20 years of preschool classroom experience bringing a wealth of knowledge in early childhood development. Her approach focuses on creating a warm, engaging, and supportive environment where children can thrive.Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 29th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, May 1st for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.Preschool Party Registration Requirements – All children who will be participating in the craft will need to be registered as a participant to allow for adequate supplies.

title: Gary Allan with guest Mark Mackay
location: PA, United States
description:
Tickets: $69 $79 $89 $99 
With the allure of a modern day outlaw, Gary Allan has won over fans, peers, and critics with his signature blend of smoldering vocals, rebellious lyrics, and raucous live performances. While becoming a force on the country music scene, Allan has remained true to his artistic voice each step of the way. The California native has released nine studio albums selling over 8 million albums, been certified platinum on three back-to-back albums, and been certified gold five times. Allan has five #1 hits on country radio and fourteen Top 10 hits to his credit. He has amassed over 2.8 billion total streams and has debuted at #1 on the Billboard Country Album chart four times, along with his SET YOU FREE album debuting at the top of the Billboard 200 (Pop Chart). He is described as “dark and dreamy” in Entertainment Weekly and deemed a “maverick” by Rolling Stone.  He sells out venues as a headliner from NY to LA and has appeared on The Tonight Show with Jay Leno, Late Show with David Letterman, Late Night with Jimmy Fallon, Live with Kelly and Michael, and Jimmy Kimmel Live. He has also landed on the covers of Country Weekly, Pollstar, and People magazine. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

We thank each family for waiting outside the Story Corner until your child's turn. Come read to a dog at Quarryville Library!  Lily and Carly will take turns coming to visit us. They love to hear kids read and won't mind if you stumble on a word here or there.  Share a story you love with a furry friend, or consider reading one of their favorite books: Go, Dog! Go! by PD EastmanNoodle and the No-Bones Day by Jonathan GrazianoCan I Be Your Dog?  by Troy CummingsHarry the Dirty Dog by Gene ZionWhere's Spot? by Eric HillClifford the Big Red Dog by Norman BridwellSheep in a Jeep by Nancy ShawThe Landis family will be coming to our library with one of their dogs (Lily and Carly) once per month and they are excited to listen to your stories. We thank them for volunteering to bring back this youth program!

title: Dick Fox’s Golden Boys starring Frankie Avalon and Fabian with special guest Tommy Cono Tributing Bobby Rydell
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $69 $79 $89 $99 
Relive the magic of the golden era of rock ‘n’ roll with Dick Fox’s Golden Boys! This unforgettable concert event reunites beloved teen idols of the late 1950s and early 1960s — Frankie Avalon and Fabian, with special guest Tommy Cono (tributing Bobby Rydell) — for an evening packed with timeless hits, charm, and nostalgia. 
From Avalon’s chart-topping favorites like “Venus” and “Why” to Fabian’s energetic classics including “Turn Me Loose” and “Tiger,” audiences will be transported back to a time when rock ‘n’ roll ruled the airwaves. With the addition of Tommy Cono’s smooth vocals and dynamic stage presence, this show is a celebration of music that defined a generation. 
Dick Fox’s Golden Boys blends unforgettable songs, stories, and humor into a joyful trip down memory lane — reminding us why these performers became household names and fan favorites around the world.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

description:

AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, August 20 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, August 22 at 10:00 a.m. 
We are excited to welcome Brothers of the Heart back to American Music Theatre! 
Super groups have a prestigious history in the music industry from such legendary rockers as The Traveling Wilburys to the iconic country foursome The Highwaymen to the stellar trio of Dolly Parton, Emmylou Harris, and Linda Ronstadt.  There’s a special excitement when successful artists combine their individual talents and star power to create something fresh and exciting.  That tradition continues with Brothers Of The Heart – Jimmy Fortune, Bradley Walker, Mike Rogers, and Ben Isaacs – who reimagine country, gospel, and pop classics with their renditions of iconic songs such as “Ring of Fire,” “Desperado,” “Bye Bye Love,” “Precious Memories,” and more.

title: Mocktails & Meaning™: Behind the Scenes of Human Trafficking
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Join us for our May 21, 2026 Mocktails & Meaning™ gathering for a powerful and eye-opening conversation led by Brad Ortenzi, Eastern USA Regional Director of ZOE International and Coordinator of the Berks and Lancaster County Human Trafficking Task Forces. Drawing on his experience as a former Ephrata Police Detective, his work with federal and county child exploitation task forces, and his international work combating trafficking in Thailand, Brad will take us behind the scenes of human trafficking in Lancaster County. This session will explore how traffickers recruit and control victims, the psychological realities of trauma bonding, and how local task forces respond through both enforcement and prevention. Time will be set aside for questions and dialogue. We’ll be serving a Mai Tai Mocktail as we engage this important and impactful conversation. 
Register By Clicking Here

Get ready for an unforgettable night of country music when Lee Brice takes the stage at American Music Theatre! Known for his powerhouse vocals, heartfelt storytelling, and chart-topping hits like “Hard to Love,” “I Don’t Dance,” “One of Them Girls,” and “Rumor,” Brice delivers a show packed with energy, emotion, and authenticity.

title: Lou Gramm: The Voice of Foreigner
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $59 $69 $79 $89
 
Get ready to relive the soundtrack of the late ’70s and ’80s when Lou Gramm, the legendary original lead singer of Foreigner, takes the stage at American Music Theatre! With one of rock’s most unmistakable voices, Gramm’s powerful vocals helped define an era, fronting chart-topping hits like “I Want to Know What Love Is,” “Cold as Ice,” “Hot Blooded,” “Juke Box Hero,” and “Double Vision.” 
In this electrifying live concert, Lou and his band deliver the energy, passion, and powerhouse vocals that made Foreigner one of the most successful rock bands of all time. Fans can expect a night packed with classics, deep cuts, and maybe a few surprises from Lou’s acclaimed solo career — including his hit “Midnight Blue.” 
Experience the timeless voice, the unforgettable songs, and the rock ’n’ roll spirit that continue to inspire generations.

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

Prepare for a soul-stirring, nostalgic concert experience at the Lovin’ Feelin’ Farewell Tour of the legendary Righteous Brothers. The Righteous Brothers Lovin’ Feelin’ Farewell Tour promises a captivating musical odyssey, spanning the early days of rock and roll, the era of Blue-Eyed Soul and as far reaching as the movie Dirty Dancing. From heartwarming ballads to energetic rock anthems, every performance is a testament to the enduring power of The Righteous Brothers’ music and their connection to fans across the globe. 
Take a journey through decades of timeless hits that have defined a generation, including “You’ve Lost That Lovin’ Feelin,'” “Unchained Melody,” “(I’ve Had) The Time of My Life,” “Soul and Inspiration,” “Ebb Tide,” and “Rock and Roll Heaven.”

title: The Happy Together Tour
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $69 $79 $89
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, January 7 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, January 9 at 10:00 a.m. 
(VIP1) HAPPY TOGETHER:  $191 
Includes: 

One Premium Reserved In Rows 2-5
One, VIP Only, Tour Poster Autographed By Graphic Artist / Designer*
Limited Availability

*Poster shipped directly to purchasers after the show has concluded *tour poster is limited, numbered & signed by the graphic artist that designed the poster** **poster autographed by graphic artist, NOT band members performing at the show.  
(VIP2) HAPPY TOGETHER: $180 
Includes: 

One Premium Reserved In Rows 5-15
One, VIP Only, Tour Poster Autographed By Graphic Artist / Designer*
Limited Availability

2026 will mark the 17th year of the highly successful, hit-filled summer package, THE “HAPPY TOGETHER” TOUR. The touring sensation that has crisscrossed the nation delighting audiences for over a decade and a half returns this summer with a show full of chart-topping hits from the ‘60s and ‘70s – an undeniable 60+ BILLBOARD Top 40 smashes. The Tour is once again joined by THE ASSOCIATION, THE TROGGS, CHICAGO lead singer 1985-2016 JASON SCHEFF, GARY PUCKETT, THE FORTUNES, RON DANTE from The Archies and The Turtles, THE VOGUES and THE COWSILLS. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Lancaster County Benefit Auction
location: Leola Produce Auction, 135 Brethren Church Road, Leola, PA, 17540, United States
description:
Experience the vibrant spirit of the annual Lancaster County Benefit Auction, a day where community fellowship meets the thrill of the auction block. This local tradition brings people together for a memorable experience filled with lively bidding and incredible food, all organized to benefit families facing rare genetic disorders at the Clinic for Special Children. 
The auction features an expansive array of goods ranging from traditional carriages and carts to masterfully crafted quilts and household furniture. Attendees can browse a massive inventory of farm supplies, hardware, and professional-grade tools, alongside outdoor furniture and a lush selection of shrubbery and plants. Whether you are searching for rare collectibles, children’s toys, or beautifully arranged theme baskets, the variety ensures there is something for every interest.

title: Lancaster County Carriage & Antique Auction
location: Bird-in-Hand Events Field, 2727 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
The Lancaster County Carriage & Antique Auction is a premier event that takes place annually on the Thursday & Friday before the last Saturday in June. There is an Early Bird Auction on Thursday afternoon and an all-day auction on Friday. 
Thousands of people come to the village of Bird-in-Hand for the auction which is held on the Smucker Homestead. Equine and antique enthusiasts, consignors, local residents, out-of-town visitors, and whole families look forward to our auction each year. Many are from the East Coast, but also from places as far away as Texas and California. 
This is a significant fundraiser for our Hand-in-Hand Fire Company. We are appreciative of the amazing support we receive both from our local volunteers and the visitors to our area who come back year after year.

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Data Lancaster Coffee Meetup
description:
Data Lancaster
We meet up at the Mean Cup coffee shop and chat about whatever topics come to mind. Attendees tend to work in data-related jobs, so expect at least some data-related discussions, and attendees sharing what they've been up to on the data-related projects they're working on is common.

Be sure to join the Data Lancaster Meetup group in order to receive notifications of future events!

*(And if you have a conflict this month and want to plan for the future, we meet the third Friday of the month every month except November and December)*

title: Bird-in-Hand Half Marathon & 5k
location: Bird-in-Hand Events Field, 2727 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
Experience a Race Like No Other.
Old-world practices. Lush countryside. Hard-working people. All these things and more make the Bird-in-Hand Half Marathon a special experience. But some things can’t be described – like the sense of community that follows well after you cross the finish line. 
Friday, September 11, 2026 

11 am – 7 pm –Race Expo & Packet Pick-up for all events
4 – 8 pm – Pre-Race Pizza & Pasta Party
6:10 pm – Kids Fun Run
6:30 pm – 5K
7:30 – 9 pm – Bonfire/S’mores Party

Saturday, September 12, 2026 

7:30 am – BIH Half Marathon
10 am – 12pm – Community Picnic

title: Free with Admission: Chemistry Magic with Eurofins Lancaster Labs
location: The Lancaster Science Factory, 454 New Holland Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Come one come all to the Chemistry Magic Show presented by real scientists from Eurofins Lancaster Laboratories! 
This dazzling show incorporates a variety of experiments to ignite your mind from homemade rocket fuel to disappearing cups, to color-changing bottles and so much more! All ages are encouraged to participate in these hands-on experiments and watch the fascinating chemical reactions. 
This activity is FREE with your paid admission or membership to the Lancaster Science Factory.

description:
Every First Friday the Lancaster Science Factory offers free admission from 5 – 7pm for the first 300 guests. Explore over 80 interactive exhibits for kids in grades K – 8 and their families! This program is possible through the generous support of PPL. 
  
CHILDREN MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY AN ADULT
